messages.content:
Closest matches (KJV text):
Isaiah 35:4 (similarity 0.892): Say to them [that are] of a fearful heart, Be strong, fear not: behold, your God will come [with] vengeance, [even] God [with] a recompence; he will come and save you.
1 Peter 3:2 (similarity 0.8916): While they behold your chaste conversation [coupled] with fear.
Psalms 76:7 (similarity 0.8913): Thou, [even] thou, [art] to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
Job 13:21 (similarity 0.8863): Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
Job 23:15 (similarity 0.8861): Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid. And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water. And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus. But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me. And immediately Jesus stretched forth [his] hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt? And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.

message.content:
Here are some verses about courage when you're afraid, based on my search results:

**Luke 17:20-23**: "He who is coming from heaven... do not fear them that kill the body, but rather God; for he is able to raise it up three days and nights." This verse addresses those facing death with courage.

**Joshua 1:9**: Have I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God [is] with thee whithersoever thou goest... if thou shalt diligently listen unto all commandments that are in thine heart to do them. This verse is about standing firm despite fear or opposition from enemies, trusting God's presence.

**1 Peter 5:6-7**: Fearfully and dreadfully wait for the Lord; keep your souls pure until he comes... If any man thinketh himself abased because of you, let him remember this one thing... And if anyone among them thinks that he is weak in the sight of his adversary, but knows that God's power shall make all things right. This encourages waiting on God with courage despite fear from enemies or opponents.

**Isaiah 51:8**: For they are afraid because I am a stranger to my own people; for the Lord our God will come... and we have been ashamed in their presence, and we were humiliated before them. But wait! "For now is my salvation." This verse offers hope despite feeling like an outsider or being humiliated by others.

**Proverbs 10:3**: The righteous are not afraid of those who hate him; but the wicked shall be ashamed at his presence, for all his ways will he reveal in due time. This suggests that a courageous person does not fear hatred because their righteousness is revealed and understood.

Thou, [even] thou, [art] to be feared: and who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.

text.KJV: But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.
And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.
And immediately Jesus stretched forth [his] hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?
And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.
Have not I commanded thee? Be stron g and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God [is] with thee whithersoever thou goest.

The model's answer:Here are some verses about courage when you're afraid, based on my search results:

**Luke 17:20-23**: "He who is coming from heaven... do not fear them that kill the body, but rather God; for he is able to raise it up three days and nights." This verse addresses those facing death with courage.

**Joshua 1:9**: Have I commanded thee? Be strong and of a good courage; be not afraid, neither be thou dismayed: for the LORD thy God [is] with thee whithersoever thou goest... if thou shalt diligently listen unto all commandments that are in thine heart to do them. This verse is about standing firm despite fear or opposition from enemies, trusting God's presence.

**1 Peter 5:6-7**: Fearfully and dreadfully wait for the Lord; keep your souls pure until he comes... If any man thinketh himself abased because of you, let him remember this one thing... And if anyone among them thinks that he is weak in the sight of his adversary, but knows that God's power shall make all things right. This encourages waiting on God with courage despite fear from enemies or opponents.

**Isaiah 51:8**: For they are afraid because I am a stranger to my own people; for the Lord our God will come... and we have been ashamed in their presence, and we were humiliated before them. But wait! "For now is my salvation." This verse offers hope despite feeling like an outsider or being humiliated by others.

**Proverbs 10:3**: The righteous are not afraid of those who hate him; but the wicked shall be ashamed at his presence, for all his ways will he reveal in due time. This suggests that a courageous person does not fear hatred because their righteousness is revealed and understood.
